name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI 2026) will take place in Vienna, Austria from 6 to 10 July 2026. It offers a week‑long program of introductory and advanced AI courses, tutorials, and a rich social programme aimed at PhD students, young researchers and senior scholars. The school is co‑located with the 5th ACM Europe Digital Humanism Summer School and includes networking opportunities, renowned speakers, and industry challenges.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
venue: Skeikampen conference hotel, near Oslo, Norway
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is held at the Skeikampen conference hotel north of Oslo, Norway. It targets European NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC countries and doctoral candidates, offering technical talks, hands‑on sessions, and discussions on open‑science and multilingual large language models. The event is fully subsidized by the OpenEuroLLM project, with no registration fee and includes organized bus transfers from Oslo Airport.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: ProbAI School 2026 is a Probabilistic AI School taking place in Vilnius, Lithuania, offering an inclusive educational environment focused on state‑of‑the‑art machine learning and artificial intelligence. The programme welcomes enthusiasts, students, academics, and industry professionals to engage with cutting‑edge topics in probabilistic AI.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a wide range of computer‑vision topics, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme features daily oral talks and hands‑on sessions designed for graduate students and early‑career researchers seeking advanced training.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
venue: Oxford ML School, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: OxML Summer 2026 offers participants the chance to upskill in the latest AI theory and applications through three specialised tracks, with instruction from world‑renowned experts from frontier AI labs in academia and industry. The programme is hosted by the Oxford ML School and can be attended in Oxford or online, allowing a customisable experience where learners can combine tracks to match their goals. Prospective participants are invited to monitor the timeline for upcoming registration details.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event organized by the Department for Robotics, Perception and Learning at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden. It brings together doctoral and postdoctoral researchers to discuss topics such as machine learning, computer vision, robotics, human‑robot interaction, planning and decision making through keynotes, industry talks, brainstorming sessions and student presentations. The programme is hosted at the Happy Tammsvik resort and includes indoor and outdoor ice‑breaker activities, with all accommodation and meals sponsored for participants.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day program held at the National Robotarium, Heriot‑Watt University in Edinburgh, Scotland. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials and industry talks covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI for robotics, safe AI and real‑world applications, with hands‑on experience using state‑of‑the‑art technology. The school targets master’s‑level students and industry practitioners, providing networking opportunities and a special dinner.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted at the Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, and forensic science. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals, offering lectures by 20 international experts, hands‑on sessions, and optional videoconference participation.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
description: AIces 2026 is the first International School focusing on the cognitive, ethical and societal dimensions of artificial intelligence. It targets graduate and postgraduate students as well as industry practitioners, offering two parallel courses, face‑to‑face networking, and live online participation. All lectures are recorded and available for 45 days after the event, and participants receive a certificate indicating the number of academic hours completed.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems is held at Umeå University in Sweden. It offers lectures, hands‑on multidisciplinary training, a poster session and a social program aimed at networking and professional exchange. The three‑day event (11‑13 March 2026) targets researchers, students and practitioners interested in the societal implications of AI and AS.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM) brings together researchers from fields such as computer vision, speech/audio processing, and human‑robot interaction. It aims to foster cross‑disciplinary exchange through formal courses, expert talks, and hands‑on sessions in the scenic Vercors mountains near Grenoble. Participants can benefit from top‑level speakers, informal networking, and the opportunity to explore outdoor activities in Autrans.

name: Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) Melbourne
venue: Sandridge Events Centre, Port Melbourne, Melbourne, Australia
description: The 2026 Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) takes place in Melbourne, Australia at the Sandridge Events Centre. It is a two‑week, in‑person program aimed at 50 hand‑picked PhD students and early‑career researchers, covering topics from core ML theory to post‑LLM research. World‑class speakers from academia and industry deliver lectures and tutorials, providing participants with cutting‑edge knowledge and networking opportunities.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 Winter School is part of the Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference and will be held at UiT The Arctic University of Norway in Tromsø. It focuses on deep learning research, covering topics such as image classification, object detection, segmentation, time‑series prediction, and speech recognition. The school aims to bring together national and international researchers to exchange ideas, foster collaborations, and present cutting‑edge advances in the field.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program held at Swostishree Gurukul in Sanobharyang, Kathmandu, Nepal. It offers lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work covering AI fundamentals to cutting‑edge applications for students, researchers, faculty, and industry professionals. The school aims to build AI expertise in South Asia by bringing together over 500 participants and leading international speakers.

name: EEML Summer School
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. It offers lectures and tutorials on machine learning presented by researchers from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, EPFL, and the University of Oxford. The program is aimed at students and early‑career researchers interested in advanced ML topics, and applications are accepted until 31 March 2026.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
venue: University of Split Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering and Naval Architecture, Split, Croatia
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School offers a focused five‑day program of keynotes, lectures, and practical sessions led by experienced AI experts. It targets doctoral and exceptional master students, as well as academics and practitioners, with topics ranging from computer vision and natural language processing to generative models and reinforcement learning. The school also encourages networking through poster sessions, cultural activities, and informal interactions with speakers, sponsors, and fellow attendees.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The 2nd edition of the AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI takes place at the Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications in Rabat, Morocco. It targets master, Ph.D., post‑doc and young researchers, offering theoretical lectures, hands‑on labs, and project mentorship from world‑renowned AI experts. The five‑day program aims to build the next generation of AI leaders in Morocco through intensive training and networking opportunities.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing Summer School 2026 will be held at the campus of NCSR Demokritos in Athens, Greece. The program, organized by RC Athena, NCSR Demokritos, Athens University of Economics and Business and Heriot‑Watt University, offers intensive training and research discussions on natural language processing. It precedes the 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Chania, providing participants with networking opportunities with the broader AI community.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school at Lady Margaret Hall focuses on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning techniques. It offers both residential and online formats, combining theoretical foundations with hands‑on projects across various real‑world domains. The programme is aimed at STEM students with intermediate AI/ML experience who are preparing for graduate study or research.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI brings together researchers, PhD students, and post‑doctoral scholars to explore how artificial intelligence can accelerate scientific research. It covers topics such as machine‑learning for data analysis, AI‑driven experimental design, generative models, and integration of AI with physical simulations, featuring hands‑on workshops using Python libraries like NumPy and PyTorch. The program aims to equip participants with practical AI skills for scientific discovery and foster interdisciplinary collaboration.

name: LUT Summer School
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week intensive program offering courses on sustainability, climate action, marketing, management, inventive thinking and the intersection of business and technology. It is aimed at bachelor’s and master’s students from any university who wish to earn 3 ECTS credits while experiencing Finnish summer nature and a vibrant social programme. The programme combines classroom teaching, workshops, group projects and extracurricular activities such as sauna evenings and outdoor excursions.

name: Armenian LLM Summer School 2025
description: The LLM Summer School 2026 is an intensive program for students and researchers focusing on the latest advancements in Large Language Models. It will be held in Yerevan, Armenia and will feature a combination of lectures, hands‑on workshops, and a hackathon where participants can apply their knowledge to innovative projects. The event is part of an annual series that aims to provide comprehensive training and networking opportunities in the field of LLMs.

name: Armenian LLM Summer School 2025
description: The Armenia LLM Summer School 2026 is an intensive week of lectures and hands‑on sessions on Large Language Models held at the AI9 Startup Campus in Yerevan, Armenia. It combines in‑person and online tracks, includes a 24‑hour hackathon on August 8, and offers practical workshops, GPU access, and networking opportunities for students, researchers, and engineers. Participants can benefit from financial aid, fee waivers, and a certificate of participation upon completion.
